reasoning: The flagged script (add-all-scripts.py) is a development tool not executed during makepkg. AUR safety focuses solely on build/install hooks.
disagreement_analysis: Qwen incorrectly flagged a development script as unsafe due to network access, while Deepseek correctly ignored non-build files.
coverage_issues: Deepseek missed add-all-scripts.py but it's irrelevant to build safety
false_positives: Qwen's add-all-scripts.py verdict: Development tool not referenced in PKGBUILD/build hooks
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Models should deprioritize scripts in .ci/, snippets/ unless referenced in PKGBUILD
Enhance context awareness: Train models to distinguish between build-time vs development-only scripts
Add build-process validation: Cross-check findings against actual PKGBUILD execution hooks
